_doc: Project config — the one home for a project's choices, so the core depends on NO specific agent. The Orchestrator resolves a seat through `roles` before spawning and reads `mode` for decision authority. A swapped-in agent is bound by its role CONTRACT (PROTOCOL.md `## Role contracts`), not by being ours — the ship-gate checks the verdict's form, not its author. This file is the single home for what used to be a separate decision-authority marker, the project-kind marker, and an implicit role wiring. Absent file or unrecognised value ⇒ the safe defaults below (mode: interactive).
_mode: autonomous | interactive — value-home for PROTOCOL.md invariant 7. Absent or unrecognised ⇒ interactive (the safe default; never an error).
_profile: full | lite | solo | yolo — the speed↔quality dial plus one named escape hatch. Value-home: PROTOCOL.md `## Project config`. Absent or unrecognised ⇒ solo (never yolo; only an explicit 'yolo' value enters the escape hatch). Guarantee profiles (full/lite/solo): the floor holds in every one — independent Reviewer, merge stamp, honesty gates, Operator's explicit merge word. Ceremony above the change's risk is a defect the Operator pays for; the dial cuts ceremony, the floor holds rigor. yolo: off-guarantee escape hatch — merge-gate off, no Reviewer required, plans are a running spec; the Operator's explicit merge word is the one floor that remains; the audit cadence is the primary compensating control.
_platform: claude | opencode — which adapter realises the contract on this project. Usually auto-detected; pinned here when needed.
_kind: code | docs | mixed — the artifact consumer type. `code`: machine-executed primary artifact. `docs`: human-read primary artifact (instructions, process docs, protocols). `mixed`: both equally (e.g. a protocol repo where docs are the deliverable and code is the transport). Seeds capability-module defaults and the reviewer framing (code/mixed → '## Code review:'; docs → '## Doc review:'). Absent or unrecognised ⇒ `code` (strict-side default, fail-safe to more rigor).
_docLanguage: Language code for this project's human-read documentation (the docs/ tree + README) — value-home for PROTOCOL.md invariant 5. Default 'en'; absent/empty/whitespace ⇒ 'en'. Pinned 'en' explicitly here on purpose: this repo's own docs are the deliverable a downstream reader consumes in English, so a dogfood flip must never translate them (the protocol's own source is English by construction regardless). Code, commits, config, and machine grammar stay English independent of this value.
_collaboration: Opt-in multi-user (team) collaboration mode. Value-home: PROTOCOL.md `## Project config`; the why: docs/decisions/multi-user-mode.md. Three sub-keys: `team` (true | false, default false — true = multi-user team mode; named `team` to avoid colliding with the `mode` and `profile` axes); `backlog` (file | forge, default file — file = today's .ai-dev/backlog.md, forge = issues replace the file); `forge` (github | gitlab | gitea | auto, default auto — names which forge's issue CLI the backlog adapter uses, auto = detect from the origin remote host). Fail-safe like every other field: an absent object, an absent key, OR an unrecognised value ⇒ the safe default (team:false, backlog:file, forge:auto) — a bad/typo'd value can NEVER silently enable team mode or forge backlog; it fails toward single-user/file. This repo is single-user dogfood, so team:false is correct here. The three readers are now wired — the `team-collaboration` capability module, the forge-issue backlog adapter (`src/adapter/forge-map.json` + orchestrator `## Backlog`), and the ship-time PR-attach of the AI-review verdict — but on a single-user project (team:false, backlog:file) they all stay inert.
_safeguards: OPTIONAL { "<rule-id>": "on" | "off" } — the GENERAL per-guard toggle. Disables individual ASK/INJECT guards: only an explicit "off" on a TOGGLEABLE rule (deny-rules.json `toggleable: true`) suppresses that one guard; everything else (absent key, "on", an unknown value, a non-toggleable or unknown id) keeps the guard ON. ONLY ask/inject guards are toggleable — a deny-class id OR a merge-gate id (merge-while-unstamped, commit-on-unstamped-main, merge-topic-unresolvable) listed "off" is IGNORED: the mechanical floor is never config-disablable (else the model could disable its own enforcer). The six toggleable guards: ssh-mutating-action, force-push, git-commit-no-verify, no-config-run-setup, no-product-brief-discover, change-route-reminder. Each "off" is a RECORDED CONSCIOUS RISK ACCEPTANCE — repo-owned, git-tracked Operator data (invariant 4), not a model bypass; toggle it via the orchestrator's `## Safeguards` side-tool or set initial states at setup. Fail-safe: absent / malformed / non-object ⇒ ALL guards on (never silently disable a gate). See PROTOCOL.md `## Enforcement`.
_modules: The enabled capability modules and their toggle values (docs/architecture.md `## Capability modules`; the catalog + per-kind defaults are homed in src/modules/registry.json). A key set to `false` turns a module OFF; an absent key resolves to the registry's per-kind default; anything else named here (an object, `true`, a malformed value) leaves it ON with the per-`kind` default merged under the override (fail-safe to MORE rigor — a bad/typo'd value can never silently disable the rigor). This repo (kind: mixed) enables `threat-model` at `rich` depth, including for non-security changes. A module is `[persona]` prompt content composed into the role agents at assembly — it sharpens reasoning, it blocks nothing mechanically.
_launch: Launch-time env — the SOURCE of truth for the settings that are NOT baked into an assembled agent. Two are models: the SESSION model (the orchestrator IS the running session, so its model is the launch model) and the GUARD model (the harness's background/small-fast model). RATIFIED option (c) with a source-of-truth refinement (docs/decisions/multi-model-setup-ux.md `## The fork`): the config is the one home and EVERY launch path consumes it — `sessionModel` is exported as ANTHROPIC_MODEL and `guardModel` as ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL into the child env BEFORE `claude` starts. The protocol launcher (src/adapter/router-launch.mjs) reads this section and exports them automatically; a personal wrapper reads the SAME values (the documented export block setup prints — src/adapter/README.md `### The launcher`). So the field is never dead cosmetics: the launch path (ours or the user's) always applies it. Default absent/empty — a non-routing project exports nothing and is byte-unchanged. These are launch ENV, never baked: changing them needs no re-bake, only a re-launch. A third optional field, `configDir`, is exported as CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR (a per-project claude profile dir — the Operator's per-task-keys mechanism, pinned per project with no .bashrc edit; useful even WITHOUT routing — docs/decisions/launcher-ux.md). A fourth, `aliases` ({ opus?, sonnet?, haiku? }), is the CROSS-ENDPOINT TIER LEVER: each tier is exported as ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_{OPUS,SONNET,HAIKU}_MODEL, binding a Claude tier to a foreign model id (e.g. sonnet → glm-4.6) so a role using that tier routes to it (the recommended cross-endpoint path — setup Stage 1/2; docs/decisions/multi-model-setup-ux.md papercut 11). Same launch-env class (startup-read, restart-applied); per-tier empty/absent ⇒ that var is unset. `aliases` is the one NESTED launch field — mergeLocalLaunch deep-merges it per-tier, so a personal config.local.json may override one tier without dropping the shared others. PERSONAL/PER-MACHINE `launch.*` values (a `configDir`, a personal launch model) belong in the GITIGNORED `.ai-dev/config.local.json` instead of this shared file — the launcher merges that file's `launch` OVER this one, so a per-machine path is never forced on a teammate; keep this shared file's `launch` for values the whole team should get. (The baked seats are roles.{builder,reviewer}.model below; there is NO roles.orchestrator.model — papercut 2.)
_roles: Each seat binds an `agent` and (for the baked seats) an optional `model`. `agent` = the id the active platform knows (a Claude subagent_type / an OpenCode agent id); swap it for ANY agent that honours the seat's contract — that is how you plug in a different reviewer (stricter, or an external engine) with zero core edit. The ONLY two baked model pins are `roles.{builder,reviewer}.model` — baked into the assembled agent at install, so changing one needs a RE-BAKE (re-run the installer). `roles.orchestrator` carries `agent` ONLY and NO `model` key: the orchestrator IS the running session, so its model is the launch model (the `launch` section above), never a config pin that nothing applies (papercut 2 — docs/decisions/multi-model-setup-ux.md). `model` states only the WISH ('session' / 'auto' / a per-platform pin); the platform adapter realises it. Omitted ⇒ 'session', EXCEPT the reviewer defaults to 'auto' (a maker-model can't see its own blind spots). The model policy — what 'auto' resolves to and each platform's model authority — is homed in the platform adapter: src/adapter/tool-map.json `models`.
